title: char.to_string() in sieve_glob_to_regex
description: sieve_glob_to_regex allocates a heap String on every character via ch.to_string() just to pass &str to fancy_regex::escape (lines 415, 417, 418, 427 in evaluator.rs). Each call does a heap allocation for a 1–4 byte string. Idiomatic Rust: use a stack-local char buffer: let mut buf = [0u8; 4]; let s = ch.encode_utf8(&mut buf); fancy_regex::escape(s). Avoids four heap allocations per pattern character.
